description_long: This is an ancient-style ceramic vase with a rounded body, a narrow neck, and a wide, flared rim. The surface features a black-figure painted scene depicting two human figures, likely from Greek mythology, bordered by a dotted band near the shoulder. The vase has a single, robust handle extending from the rim to the shoulder, and the base is slightly raised and circular. The exterior shows significant wear, with visible cracks, chips, and areas where the paint has faded or worn away, revealing the underlying clay. The overall coloration is a mix of terracotta orange and black, with the painted figures and decorative bands providing visual interest.
description: A weathered, ancient-style ceramic vase with a flared rim, single handle, and black-figure painted scene on a terracotta background.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed black-figure scene with two human figures and decorative banding.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the single handle and the transition from painted to unpainted surface.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the unadorned, worn surface and the full length of the handle.
description_view_3: Angled view showing both the handle and a partial glimpse of the painted scene.
